what_it_does: Records a channel attachment that links attachment id att-e0a540c2 in the provider-registry channel to board item item-16cd0303, with attachment_type board_items and an added timestamp.
observations: Attachment metadata is stored as YAML front matter with no body content.
The attachment belongs to the provider-registry channel.
The target is a board item URI board://item-16cd0303.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to reference a board item.
The file is purely relational metadata with no prose or code.
conventions: Uses YAML front matter for structured metadata.
Attachment filenames match the id field.
References external entities with a channel-scoped URI scheme.
concerns: No content beyond metadata; purpose depends entirely on external board item and channel definitions.
notes: This is a tiny relational ledger entry connecting a provider-registry attachment to a specific board item.
questions: What content or relationship does board item item-16cd0303 represent?
Is the board:// URI resolvable within the channel-memory system?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as a link record between an attachment and a board item, which matches its content; no stated purpose is present beyond the metadata fields.
intention_signals: It seems intended to register that a particular attachment is associated with a board item in the provider-registry channel, enabling lookup by channel, attachment type, and target.
novelty: The board:// URI scheme used as a target is a distinctive cross-reference convention.
contribution: Provides a concrete, timestamped association between an attachment identifier and a board item in the provider-registry channel, which a fused system could retain as a provenance/relationship record.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record linking attachment att-e0a540c2 to board item item-16cd0303 in the provider-registry channel with type board_items.
intention_for_embedding: To record that a provider-registry attachment of type board_items targets a specific board item at a known time.
